title: epipactis helleborine subsp. turcica
intro: Epipactis helleborine subsp. turcica, also known as the Turkish Helleborine, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the Helleborine orchid native to the mountainous regions of Turkey. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Epipactis helleborine subsp. turcica include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy in humans.
